behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module boundary (request builder -> API client handoff), so a pure Unit test of string/prompt construction can miss dropped fields at dispatch. Real OS/network is not required, and correctness does not depend on model output content. Rejected System because behavior text only requires payload composition/forwarding, not real HTTP transport effects.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a stub/fake API client and assert the actual dispatched request payload contains the full taxonomy list for multiple taxonomies.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is local normalization/validation logic on one response value. No real infrastructure or cross-module interaction is required to expose the failure. Rejected Integration because behavior text targets one mapping rule, not a handoff-dependent state bug.
behaviors.test_description: Call the label-normalization function with an out-of-taxonomy label and assert output is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Determinism/idempotence of normalization is a pure function property. No I/O, no real API, and no orchestration boundary are needed. Rejected Integration because behavior text is explicitly about the normalization step itself.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke normalization repeatedly with the same raw response fixture and assert the resulting label is identical across runs.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on repeated live model outputs and their stability; stubbed responses hide real non-determinism and model-side variation. Real HTTP is involved, but the critical failure boundary is model decision variability, so Agentic is the minimum over System. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification checks, not a multi-stage agent pipeline with sequential decision handoffs.
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated real provider classifications for fixed (document, taxonomy), log provider raw outputs and final labels, and assert label drift only occurs when raw provider outputs differ.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: The failure is commonly at handoff/order: budget check may exist but be bypassed before send. Need module wiring (budget policy + dispatcher) to catch real failure. No real OS/network required, and no dependence on model output quality. Rejected System because behavior text is pre-dispatch enforcement, which can be validated with in-process fakes.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with real budget-enforcement path and fake API client; verify over-budget inputs are truncated/rejected per policy and that forbidden dispatches do not reach the client.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real provider token accounting and real API responses; mocks can always return compliant usage and hide true over-budget behavior. Not primarily model-quality dependent (semantic correctness), so Agentic is not required. Rejected Integration because behavior text requires provider-reported usage, which is an external-system boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real API calls for representative documents and assert provider-reported usage metadata is <= configured budget for each call.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is retry orchestration across local modules (error handling, backoff, retry loop, final return path). This can be reliably tested with a fake client that emits rate-limit signals then success. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real network timing/pathologies, only correct handling of the rate-limit signal and retry behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process fake API client scripted to return rate-limit errors before success; assert the module performs wait/backoff and eventually returns success instead of surfacing the initial error.
